programmes.show_description: Giant sturgeons are farmed for the delicacy of Russian caviar. Workers create soccer cleats worn by the sport's greatest players.
Danish bomb squads find and dispose of thousands of World War II landmines. Weed eaters chop down growth with just a sliver of nylon.
McLaren P1. Mike and Elvis offer their expertise to a Youtuber on his ambitious McLaren P1 build. Can the boys help build the fastest McLaren in the world?
Mini - UK. In their most ambitious project yet, Mike and Elvis aim to build a tribute to Paddy Hopkirk's winning 1964 Mini Cooper. Will it cross the finish line?
West Coast Of Cornwall. Henry and Sam are on the west coast of Cornwall. Sifting through 50 years of vintage vehicles, they find the first-ever Lotus Elan and a pre-war Sunbeam.
Oxfordshire. Henry and Sam are in Oxfordshire, checking out a shed full of oversized items. Henry likes a tractor in blue and Sam falls for a Midget racer in a hedge.
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects including promotional origami and hedge shears made?
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects including barbeque utensils made?
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Geoff is asked to repair a pinball machine that hasn't been used in over 20 years. Will works on a rocking horse, while Steve fixes a barograph.
In Fulham, Drew prepares to spend big with some elite dealers. In Hastings, Drew and Tee unearth some unusual Chinese watercolours and a Turkish rug.
A Chipping Norton barbershop hides an incredible antiques emporium. In an Anglesey salt factory, Drew and Tee discover an unusual bread and cheese cupboard.
The Dirt Dogs take on the biggest gamble of their career, and the Gold Gypsies tear into an all-or-nothing final mission. Meanwhile Rick digs deep to save his season.
The Dirt Dogs go to Kalgoorlie to process the gold from their Heap Leach. Meanwhile, Ted and Lecky's daughter, model and jewellery designer Tyler, joins their hunt for pay dirt.
After an injury to one of their members, the Young Guns face a tough decision on how to continue mining. Plus, the Cheals break through to a long-lost underground mine.
The Bushmen combat opal thieves on land and in the skies. Plus, Lightning Ridge's Gavan McFarlane puts his son to work and the Boulder Boys dig their way out of trouble.
Geoff is asked to repair a pinball machine that hasn't been used in over 20 years. Will works on a rocking horse, while Steve fixes a barograph.
Somerset. Henry and Sam scour Britain's sheds for vehicles to buy, restore and flog for a tidy profit. In Somerset they find Norton motorbikes, Italian mopeds, and French go-karts.
Hertfordshire. The guys are in Hertfordshire, finding vintage cars, tractors and rare British bikes from the 1950s. When the haggling starts, things don't turn out quite as expected.
Dave / Lincolnshire. In Lincolnshire, Henry and Fuzz trawl through Dave's eclectic mix of military, classic and agricultural items. What will they come away with?
Tim / East Midlands. Henry and Fuzz meet Tim, a specialist bike restorer with a passion for all things British. Will they persuade him to part with a rare Triumph soft top?
X Marks The Spot. Angus helps a lady sell her parents' belongings and finds treasure with a rare military map. Metal detecting finds and jewellery dazzle on sale day.
Cashing In On Cutlery? Angus helps an antiques dealer clear her home of treasures to fund a holiday. He also assists a woman sell her late uncle's collection of spoons.
Satire, Scent And Salt Bags. Natasha Raskin Sharp and Ishy Khan's head to North Yorkshire, where they find a satirical Georgian caricature, salt bags and a glass terrarium.
Big Hairy Pals. In East Yorkshire, Natasha snaps up a Scottish Arts and Crafts potpourri bowl, and Ishy scoops up a glass terrier inkwell. Who will come out on top?
H Is For Help! With the workshop haemorrhaging money, Richard brings in help. A merchandising scheme backfires when Lewis massively over-orders stock.
No Risk No Reward. The Opal Lovers excavate old drives in search of red crystal opal. Plus, mechanical problems are once again giving the Mooka Boys a headache.
Place Your Bets! Andrew and Mish hunt for coins in Victoria's Golden Triangle. Michael and Max encounter one of the world's deadliest marine creatures.
A big-top circus tent is erected as high as a building in less than 36 hours. Plus, Japanese workers create handcrafted guitars.
A 700-year-old tree is turned into a Japanese drum. A tech factory builds thousands of cars while staying green. Two ingredients are made into pasta.
The centuries-old secrets behind making frankfurters are revealed. Plus, sniper rifles are made to hit a target from a thousand yards.
The world's biggest fish market feeds Japan's insatiable appetite for seafood. Plus, the trunk of a giant tree is transformed into cricket bats.
Tony's Dream Land Rover 2A. Car dealer supremo Mike Brewer and mechanic Marc 'Elvis' Priestley trade up to an iconic off roader, a Series 2A Land Rover, for ex-soldier Tony.
Chris's Dream Ford Escort Mk2. Car dealer supremo Mike Brewer and mechanic Marc 'Elvis' Priestley are on a mission to trade up to a classic Mk2 Ford Escort for office manager, Chris.
Somerset. Henry and Sam scour Britain's sheds for vehicles to buy, restore and flog for a tidy profit. In Somerset they find Norton motorbikes, Italian mopeds, and French go-karts.
Hertfordshire. The guys are in Hertfordshire, finding vintage cars, tractors and rare British bikes from the 1950s. When the haggling starts, things don't turn out quite as expected.
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects including boat radar mounts made?
A look at how everyday items including Asian noodles and rotary engines are made, revealing the unique production processes used in making them.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Bicycle expert Tim is tasked with repairing a rusty penny-farthing. Leather worker Suzie restores a briefcase, while Steve renovates a clocking-in clock.
Drew's son Tom is keen to learn more about the trade. They visit Shepton Mallet Flea market in Somerset, where Tom spots a stunning painting.
Drew's ability to work under pressure is tested by talented dealer, Alasdair. In a disused church, Drew bids on an elaborate Victorian mirror.
The Dirt Dogs are determined to get the gold-tally up after last year's devastating season. Meanwhile, the Scrappers are stuck building their machine leaving them behind schedule.
The Dirt Dogs are under more pressure when their plant doesn't deliver the gold they're expecting. Meanwhile, the Scrappers are in trouble before they've even started mining.
The Bushmen stay up late to drive out poachers from their mine. Plus, Col Duff reunites with Greg in one last push to find opal before the summer heat gets too much.
The Young Guns risk it all to find rare pineapple opal. Meanwhile, the Boulder Boys take on deadly ground and the Bushmen face a race to the hospital.
Bicycle expert Tim is tasked with repairing a rusty penny-farthing. Leather worker Suzie restores a briefcase, while Steve renovates a clocking-in clock.
Cotswolds. Henry and Sam are in the Cotswolds, where they discover sheds full of stationary engines, obscure agricultural implements and some surprising collections of memorabilia.
West Sussex. Henry and Sam uncover vintage treasures in West Sussex. They go behind the scenes at one of Britain's most famous bike shops and find two extraordinary hidden gems.
Dave / Cumbria. Henry and Fuzz head for Cumbria where Dave's mega-shed awaits. Henry is lost for words but once the haggle begins, what will he decide on?
George / Dorset. Henry and Fuzz explore George's automobilia collection in Dorset. Henry's in the mood for a SPQR: Small Profit Quick Return. Can he strike a good deal?
He Shoots, He Scores. Angus visits an ex-soldier with a collection of vintage Action Man figures. A football fanatic wins big when he sells his Crystal Palace memorabilia.
Battle Stations. Angus is called in to auction a massive collection of 20,000 model soldiers. And a woman needs to sell her colossal doll's house before retiring.
A Rebel Without A Cause? Natasha and Ishy take on the penultimate leg of their trip. Natasha buys a couple of antique jugs, and Ishy purchases a beautiful Art Nouveau charger.
The Witching Hour. Natasha Raskin Sharp and Ishy Khan kick their final leg off in Derbyshire. With a spectacular auction finale in Bristol, who will come out on top?
The Beaver & A Russian Egg. Angus uncovers expensive oak and a Fabergé egg in Herefordshire, while rare single malt whiskey means big bids and spirited surprises!
Patricia visits silversmith Brendon in the hopes he can restore an old family brush and comb set. Meanwhile, Dominic's skills are put to the ultimate test.
A music expert attempts to save a vintage guitar that hasn't been played in 40 years. Meanwhile, Steve brings a well-travelled time piece back to life.
Powerboats are built to ride waves faster than cars on the road. The secrets behind Swiss watches are revealed. Workers create high-quality wallpaper.
The world's leading submarine rescue specialists from several countries participate in a training exercise. Workers manufacture all-wheel drive cars.
